bug_report.summary: Top Sites hover title reveal does not show the full long title (tooltip text is cut off and wraps mid-word)
steps_to_reproduce: Open a New Tab page.
Create/add a Top Site with a very long Title (longer than can fit under the tile).
Hover the mouse over the created Top Site tile.
expected_behaviors: Hover UI should allow the full Title text to be read (wrapping as needed).
Text wrapping should not split words awkwardly (e.g., breaking in the middle of a word), and the full string should be accessible without being cut off.
actual_behaviors: The hover title reveal bubble shows only a partial portion of the long title (the text stops before the end of the entered title).
Words appear broken across lines mid-word (e.g., “reve\nal”, “fu\nlly”), reducing readability and suggesting the tooltip/reveal container is too narrow or incorrectly wrapped.

bug_report.summary: New Shortcut modal preview tile layout is broken: long title wraps into an extremely narrow word-per-line column
steps_to_reproduce: Open New Tab.
Open ‘Add Shortcut’ to show the ‘New Shortcut’ modal.
Enter a long Title (as in the scenario).
expected_behaviors: Preview should show the title at a normal tile width, wrapping to a reasonable number of lines or truncating consistently.
actual_behaviors: Preview renders the title in an abnormally narrow column with excessive wrapping (nearly one word per line), making it hard to read.

bug_report.summary: New Shortcut modal cannot be submitted: Enter/Return and/or clicking “Add” does not close the dialog or visibly create the shortcut
steps_to_reproduce: Open New Tab.
Open the “New Shortcut” modal (Add Shortcut).
Fill Title and URL fields.
Press Enter/Return (and/or click the “Add” button).
expected_behaviors: Dialog submits (equivalent to clicking Add), closes, and the new Top Site/shortcut appears in Top Sites.
actual_behaviors: Modal remains open after submission attempts; no visible completion (no dismiss/creation) is shown.

false_positive_reasons: GUI element location or interaction error (non-deterministic behavior)

bug_report.summary: Newly added Top Site title is prefixed with an unexpected star character ("★") instead of matching the entered title text
steps_to_reproduce: Open a new tab (New Tab page).
Click the Add Shortcut (+) tile in Top Sites.
In the New Shortcut dialog, enter Title: "TS01 Mozilla" and URL: "https://www.mozilla.org/".
Click "Add".
expected_behaviors: The created Top Site tile label should match the entered title text (e.g., "TS01 Mozilla", possibly truncated with ellipsis if needed) without extra characters.
actual_behaviors: The created tile label appears as "★ TS01 Mo..." (leading star character added), which does not match the entered title.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering

bug_report.summary: Cannot add more than 8 Top Sites: “Add Shortcut” (+) control disappears after filling the row, blocking adding the required 10 tiles
steps_to_reproduce: Open a New Tab page.
Use the Add Shortcut (+) tile to add a new Top Site (e.g., Title “TS01 Mozilla”, URL “https://www.mozilla.org/”), then click Add.
Observe the Top Sites section after the tile is added.
expected_behaviors: An Add Shortcut/Add Top Site control remains available (e.g., a '+' tile or equivalent) so additional Top Sites can be added beyond the first row, allowing the grid to wrap into multiple rows (as required by the test scenario).
actual_behaviors: After the row reaches 8 tiles, the '+' Add Shortcut tile is no longer visible, and the available “...” menu on a tile only shows Pin/Edit/Open/Dismiss options—no way to add further Top Sites from the Top Sites UI.

bug_report.summary: Nightly security warning infobar no longer appears on New Tab in after build (layout shifts upward)
steps_to_reproduce: Launch Firefox Nightly with a profile that shows the Nightly security warning infobar on New Tab (as in the before build).
Open a New Tab (Ctrl+T).
Observe the area directly under the toolbar/address bar.
expected_behaviors: The Nightly warning infobar (“Some of Nightly’s security features may offer less protection…”, with actions like “How to fix this issue” / “Don’t show again”) should appear consistently on New Tab unless the user explicitly dismisses it.
Page layout should not shift due to the infobar being unexpectedly absent.
actual_behaviors: In the after build, the warning infobar is not shown on New Tab, and the New Tab content (Firefox logo/search/Top Sites) is shifted upward compared to the before build.

false_positive_reasons: Code-induced GUI difference leading to replay mismatch

bug_report.summary: New Tab customization/settings panel fails to render (or is empty) after clicking the gear icon in before build
steps_to_reproduce: Open a New Tab page.
Click the settings gear icon on the New Tab page (top-right of the content area).
expected_behaviors: The New Tab customization panel opens and displays controls such as Wallpapers, Shortcuts, and Recommended stories options.
actual_behaviors: The customization panel content does not appear in the before build (right side shows blank/empty overlay regions instead of the Wallpapers/Shortcuts/Recommended stories controls).

false_positive_reasons: Code-induced GUI difference leading to replay mismatch

bug_report.summary: New Tab unexpectedly scrolls/jumps to top when closing a context menu with Escape
steps_to_reproduce: Open a New Tab page.
Scroll down so the “Thought-provoking stories” cards are in view.
Right-click a story card to open the context menu.
Press Escape to close the context menu.
expected_behaviors: Context menu closes and the page remains at the same scroll position (Stories stay in view where the user was).
actual_behaviors: After closing the context menu, the page/viewport jumps upward (search field + Top Sites area become visible), shifting the content position instead of staying on the Stories section.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering

bug_report.summary: Edit Shortcut dialog preview title wraps into an extremely narrow column (one-word-per-line) after entering a long title
steps_to_reproduce: Open a New Tab page.
Create or edit a Top Site/Shortcut so the “Edit Shortcut” dialog is shown.
In the Title field, enter a very long title (multiple words/sentence).
expected_behaviors: The preview of the tile title in the dialog should display with normal wrapping/ellipsis consistent with Top Sites tiles (e.g., limited lines or sensible width), without producing excessive vertical growth.
actual_behaviors: The preview title renders in an abnormally narrow column, wrapping almost every word onto its own line, producing an excessively tall, hard-to-read text block in the dialog.

bug_report.summary: Edit Shortcut dialog URL field drops the "https://" protocol after tabbing/focus change
steps_to_reproduce: Open New Tab.
Create a Top Site/Shortcut with a full URL including protocol (e.g., "https://www.ubuntu.com/?edit-title-test").
Open the tile menu and choose Edit to open the Edit Shortcut dialog.
Press Tab to move focus within the dialog (e.g., into the URL field).
expected_behaviors: The URL field should continue to display/preserve the full URL including the protocol exactly as entered/stored (e.g., starting with "https://").
actual_behaviors: The URL field shows the URL without the protocol prefix (e.g., "www.ubuntu.com/?edit-title-test"), indicating the value may have been altered/normalized unexpectedly.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering

bug_report.summary: Edit Shortcut dialog: pressing Enter (intended to activate Save) opens Custom Image URL controls instead of saving
steps_to_reproduce: Open New Tab.
Open a Top Site tile menu and choose Edit to open the Edit Shortcut dialog.
Use keyboard navigation (Tab) to reach the Save action (or attempt to) and press Enter.
expected_behaviors: Enter activates the Save button (when Save is focused) and closes the dialog, applying the changes.
actual_behaviors: After pressing Enter, the dialog remains open and a “Custom Image URL” section/field appears, consistent with activating “Use a custom image…” instead of saving.

false_positive_reasons: GUI element location or interaction error (non-deterministic behavior)

bug_report.summary: Edit Shortcut dialog: Save/Cancel actions disappear from view when “Custom Image URL” section is shown
steps_to_reproduce: Open a New Tab page.
Open a Top Site tile menu and choose Edit (open Edit Shortcut dialog).
Reveal the “Custom Image URL” section (e.g., activate “Use a custom image…”).
expected_behaviors: Primary actions (Save/Cancel) remain visible and reachable in the dialog (typically fixed in the footer), even when extra sections like “Custom Image URL” are displayed.
actual_behaviors: With “Custom Image URL” visible, the dialog footer actions (Save/Cancel) are not visible in the modal, implying they are pushed out of view and may require scrolling or may be inaccessible.
